title: Dream bars
subtitle: American bar cookies with a coconut and brown sugar topping
excerpt: Dream bars are American bar cookies with a shortbread base and a coconut, brown sugar, and nut topping that bakes into a sticky, chewy layer. They are one of the classic American church-supper and bake-sale bars: portable, easy to cut, and better at room temperature than warm. The name dates to at least the 1940s.
sourceNotes: Dream bars are one of a family of American layered bar cookies that appear widely in mid-twentieth-century domestic recipe collections, particularly in community cookbooks, church publications, and women's magazines from the 1940s onward. The formula of a shortbread base with a coconut and brown sugar topping is consistent across many regional variants, sometimes called Hello Dolly bars, seven-layer bars, or magic bars depending on additional ingredients. The basic two-layer version is the original.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cut bars for up to 2 months. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make a day ahead; the topping sets more firmly overnight and the bars cut more cleanly.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 25 to 32 minutes; the topping should be set and deep amber, the base pale gold underneath

content.text: Dream bars are made in two stages: press the shortbread base into the tin and bake briefly until just set, then spread the topping over the hot base and return to the oven. The topping is a simple mix of brown sugar, eggs, coconut, and chopped nuts that sets into a dense, fudgy layer. The bars need to cool completely before cutting: the topping is still liquid when hot and will not slice cleanly until it has set firm.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C fan. Line a 30 x 20 cm tin with baking paper. Beat 115 g softened butter with 50 g icing sugar until combined. Mix in 150 g plain flour until a soft, slightly crumbly dough forms. Press evenly into the base of the tin. Bake for 12 minutes until just set and very lightly coloured at the edges.

While the base bakes, beat 2 large eggs with 200 g dark brown sugar and 1 tsp vanilla extract. Stir in 2 tablespoons of plain flour and half a teaspoon of baking powder, then fold in 100 g desiccated coconut and 75 g roughly chopped walnuts. Pour over the hot base and spread to the edges. Return to the oven and bake for 18 to 20 minutes until the topping is set, deep amber, and does not wobble in the centre. Cool completely in the tin before cutting.

items.problem: The topping is sticky and will not set
items.solution: The bars need more time in the oven, or were cut before they had cooled fully. The topping continues to firm up as it cools. Leave in the tin for at least 30 minutes, or refrigerate for 15 minutes to speed the process.

items.problem: The base is soggy under the topping
items.solution: The base was not prebaked long enough before the topping was added. It should be just set and faintly coloured at the edges before the wet topping goes on.
